title: dischidia boholensis
intro: Dischidia boholensis, also known as the Philippine climbing milkwort, is a type of epiphytic plant that belongs to the family Apocynaceae. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues due to its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Dischidia boholensis include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and protect against oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
